exercises.title: String Length and Indexing
exercises.description: Practice len() and indexing on the given string. Print the length, then the first character, then the last character.
exercises.starterCode:
word = "Python"

# TODO: Print the length of word
# TODO: Print the first character (index 0)
# TODO: Print the last character (index -1)
hints: len(word) returns the number of characters.
word[0] is the first character; word[-1] is the last.

exercises.title: Slicing and Reversal
exercises.description: Use slicing to extract parts of a string and reverse it. Given the sentence below, print the first word, print the last 3 characters, and print the whole string reversed.
exercises.starterCode:
sentence = "Hello World"

# TODO: Print the first 5 characters ('Hello')
# TODO: Print the last 5 characters ('World')
# TODO: Print the sentence reversed

exercises.title: String Methods
exercises.description: Apply common string methods to transform text. Use the given name and apply upper(), lower(), title(), and replace() as instructed.
exercises.starterCode:
name = "  alice johnson  "

# TODO: Print name with leading/trailing spaces removed (strip)
# TODO: Print name stripped and then converted to uppercase
# TODO: Print name stripped and then converted to title case
# TODO: Print the length of name (stripped)
exercises.expectedOutput:
alice johnson
ALICE JOHNSON
Alice Johnson
13
hints: name.strip() removes leading and trailing whitespace.
title() capitalizes the first letter of each word.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: String Operations
warmup.description: Strings support + (concatenation) and * (repetition). What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
first = "Hello"
second = "World"
print(first + ", " + second + "!")
print("-" * 15)
print(len("Python"))
